reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider GHK-Cu as a chronic, daily, subcutaneous-only signal peptide rather than a short cosmetic course, with an upper-bound disagreement that must be triaged before any prescription: Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:04:53.680): "minimum one, but I'd say two, two milligrams of this peptide every day" while Hunter (GLOW protocol, 2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily" of a 50mg/10mg/10mg GLOW blend (≈1.67 mg GHK-Cu/day) and Taylor (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:10:54.880): "for beauty enhancement I like to do a dose of 1.5 MLs of it" with up to 3 mL for recovery. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh route (subq only, with topical as an option for post-surgical irritation per Hunter 4igc2UpFr7Q 00:25:18.160) and stacking conflicts because Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:13:38.720): "Administer GHK-Cu alone. Period."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Subcutaneous-only and burn at injection site: Taylor (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:08:00.680): "this peptide is one that you are going to in you are going to inject it um" and (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:09:51.600): "if you also take bpc 157 with your ghk um a lot of people say that that actually helps um with any kind of pain with injecting"; Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:39:29.680): "GHK burns like GHK if you injected burns"; Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:25:18.160): "if you tolerate it well, you can do fine, or you can do some oral GHK, some top- or not oral, some topical GHK on the site"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:16:38.480): "I do this even if I'm just taking ghk by itself because for me it does burn" — her workaround is reconstituting in 6 mL bacteriostatic water rather than 3 mL.
- Do NOT mix in same syringe with other peptides: Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:13:38.720): "Administer GHK-Cu alone. Period. By itself. If you're taking any other other peptides 15 to 20 minutes even 30 minutes space them apart like that"; Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:13:01.120): "The copper ion in GHK-Cu is highly reactive. It's a transition metal"; Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:36:34.000): "until there's actually studies on the stability, we don't really know." Note this contradicts the GLOW blend product (which is pre-blended in vial); Hunter still endorses GLOW (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775).
- Chronic-cancer caution: Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:33:20.480): "peptides when used longterm cause cancer. That's the big concern" and (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:33:33.120): "you want to screen people before you don't want to use it in active cancer. you don't want to use it long term because of that"; Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:33:38.320): "tumor angiogenesis is not the same as physiological angiogenesis."
- Post-surgical context: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:24:30.160): "I would probably stay stop a few days uh before, and then maybe just one or two days after to kind of let the inflammation come in uh and do what it needs to do for the body, and then I would just go right into those after."
- CJC saturation note (Hunter's framing of overdose risk applies broadly): Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:38:54.400): "there's like a saturation point beyond which it doesn't really matter how much you take and actually it could probably be the inverse. It could actually probably have a negative effect."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Subcutaneous injection is the primary route across all three. Hunter (2025-08-29 threadId 198f7e2e34be4381): "For tissue remodeling, recovery support, or cosmetic skin targets, I like small daily micro-doses such as 5–10 units subcutaneously near the target area"; Taylor (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:08:00.680): "this peptide is one that you are going to inject it"; Trevor (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:18:34.240): "200 to 300 micrograms every day subq." Skin/connective-tissue/wound mechanism is unanimous: Hunter (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "GHK-Cu is the master painter and interior designer. It restores the surface, smooths out the scars, and keeps everything looking and feeling young"; Taylor (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:01:38.040): "this peptide also helps development of the connected tissue" and (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:02:08.080): "help with the antioxidants and inflammation properties"; Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:09:50.000): "it increases copper alone increases the production of mitochondria" and (xxTZXHynh20 00:09:58.520): "GHK-Cu doesn't just mop up a few free radicals. It supercharges your body's own built-in defense system." Daily-dosing frame: Hunter GLOW protocol (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily, morning or evening"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:27:30.200): "5 days a week of this um blend"; Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:11:51.400): "just take it forever and it gives you the optimal health."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Daily dose magnitude differs by an order of magnitude. Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:04:53.680): "minimum one, but I'd say two, two milligrams of this peptide every day" and (xxTZXHynh20 00:30:11.880): "a non-negotiable base of 1 to 2 mg"; older Trevor (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:18:34.240): "200 to 300 micrograms every day subq" — Trevor's newer 2025-10-22 content overrides his earlier 2025-07-18 200-300 mcg framing as 10x too low. Hunter's GLOW protocol (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775) yields ≈1.67 mg GHK-Cu per 10-unit daily dose, landing inside Trevor's 1-2 mg window, but Hunter (2025-08-29 threadId 198f7e2e34be4381): "5–10 units is already a meaningful dose" allows starting as low as ≈833 mcg/day. Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:15:44.120): "if I want take 2.5 mg that would be 15 units" — her glow-blend pull lands at 2.5 mg of total blend, of which ≈71% (≈1.78 mg) is GHK-Cu. Cycling/duration differs sharply: Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:10:35.720): "you should take it forever hypothetically for your experiment" and (xxTZXHynh20 00:10:43.280): "Are you out of your mind? Okay, you cycle it. Let me get this straight. You don't cycle oxygen do you? Do you cycle water?" — this is the FOREVER frame. Older Trevor (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:51:15.119): "GHK GHKU the copper TB500 and BPC157 8 to 12 weeks on then just pulse" — 8-12-week cycling frame. Hunter (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "You can run this for 20 days (one vial per cycle), and repeat as needed" and (2025-08-29 threadId 198f7e2e34be4381): "evaluate response over 4–8 weeks"; Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:34:09.440): "don't just use it forever" — Hunter's CYCLE frame. Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:28:55.760): "if you're using this for an injury recovery I would keep using this until you were completely healed" and (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:30:18.799): "as far as the beauty side of it goes and the aesthetic side I would use it for a good 6 to 12 weeks"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:29:45.559): "if you only do it for like 6 weeks or 8 weeks and you're still in a healing recovery time like it actually and you go back to use it in a couple weeks it actually can like prevent it from not being able to keep further and longer healing recovery" — Taylor's GOAL-BASED frame (heal-to-completion or 6-12 weeks). Stacking-in-syringe also differs: Trevor (xxTZXHynh20 00:13:38.720): "Administer GHK-Cu alone. Period" while Hunter ships the GLOW pre-blend (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775) AND simultaneously (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:37:09.120): "I would actually prefer if I'm using those for a certain effect to rotate through them almost like I'm going to do BPC maybe even if it's just like a weekly thing BPC for a week then TB the next week and then GHK the next week."

acute.dose: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:24:08.160) names GHK as a post-surgical / acute-recovery substitute when BPC-157 is contraindicated: "we have TB-500, we have cardarine, we have GHK, we have PEG-MGF"; Hunter (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "For targeted healing (painful knees, shoulder, surgery site), inject near the affected area (but not directly into the injury—always around, not in)" at 10 units of GLOW (≈1.67 mg GHK-Cu); Taylor (zd2eGg-0-OI 00:10:58.560): "if you are somebody who were trying to do more of a recovery you could go up to three" (3 mL pull from her 50mg-in-3mL reconstitution).
acute.route: Subcutaneous near the target area: Hunter (2025-08-29 threadId 198f7e2e34be4381): "5–10 units subcutaneously near the target area, then evaluate response over 4–8 weeks"; topical alternative for post-surgical irritation: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:25:18.160): "if you tolerate it well, you can do fine, or you can do some oral GHK, some top- or not oral, some topical GHK on the site"
acute.frequency: Daily — Hunter (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily, morning or evening, your choice"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:27:30.200): "5 days a week"
acute.duration: Hunter (2025-07-26 threadId 198489a54128a775): "You can run this for 20 days (one vial per cycle), and repeat as needed, especially during injury recovery"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:28:55.760): "if you're using this for an injury recovery I would keep using this until you were completely healed"

Now, if you tolerate it well, you can do fine, or you can do some oral GHK, some top- or not oral, some topical GHK on the site to help with that."
